strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance in long-horizon agent memory.
Novel method: claim-level memory with explicit temporal and contradiction edges.
Strong empirical claim: +40pp on temporal reasoning, -58% tokens vs. baseline.
Well-structured ablation isolating the contribution of truth-maintenance edges.
Good clarity in describing method components and experimental setup.
weaknesses: Results rely heavily on placeholder macros; actual performance unverified.
Ablation results incomplete (e.g., '--' values for key metrics).
Cross-domain results (LoCoMo, MAB-CR) not reported (shown as '--').
Method details (e.g., greedy max-consistent subgraph) lack theoretical justification.
Comparison to A-Mem reimplementation may lack reproducibility details.
No discussion of limitations or failure cases of TTMG.
